smile_attr_var.name: smile_attr_var
smile_attr_var.description: Function-style macro to return a variable attribute of a dataset. The following attributes are available: VARTYPE, VARLEN, VARLABEL, VARFMT and VARINFMT
smile_attr_var.reference: Main programming parts are coming from attrv.sas macro from Roland Rashleigh-Berry who has published his code under the unlicence license in his utility package (http://www.datasavantconsulting.com/roland/Spectre/download.html)
smile_attr_var.author: Katja Glass

parameters.ATTRIB: SAS variable attrib keyword (e.g. VARTYPE, VARLEN, VARLABEL, VARFMT and VARINFMT)

smile_attrc.name: smile_attrc
smile_attrc.description: Function-style macro to return a character attribute of a dataset. The following attributes are available: CHARSET, COMPRESS, DATAREP, ENCODING, ENCRYPT, ENGINE, LABEL, LIB, MEM, MODE, MTYPE, SORTEDBY, SORTLVL, SORTSEQ, TYPE
smile_attrc.reference: Main programming parts are coming from attrc.sas macro from Roland Rashleigh-Berry who has published his code under the unlicence license in his utility package (http://www.datasavantconsulting.com/roland/Spectre/download.html)
smile_attrc.author: Katja Glass

parameters.ATTRIB: SAS ATTRC keyword (e.g. TYPE, LIB, LABEL, SORTEDBY, ...)

smile_attrn.name: smile_attrn
smile_attrn.description: Function-style macro to return a numeric attribute of a dataset. The following attributes are available: ALTERPW, ANOBS, ANY, ARAND, ARWU, AUDIT, AUDIT_DATA, AUDIT_BEFORE, AUDIT_ERROR, CRDTE, ICONST, INDEX, ISINDEX, ISSUBSET, LRECL, LRID, MAXGEN, MAXRC, MODTE, NDEL, NEXTGEN, NLOBS, NLOBSF, NOBS, NVARS, PW, RADIX, READPW, REUSE, TAPE, WHSTMT, WRITEPW
smile_attrn.reference: Main programming parts are coming from attrn.sas macro from Roland Rashleigh-Berry who has published his code under the unlicence license in his utility package (http://www.datasavantconsulting.com/roland/Spectre/download.html)
smile_attrn.author: Katja Glass

smile_ods_document_flat_label.name: smile_ods_document_flat_label
smile_ods_document_flat_label.description: Flat navigation and optionally re-label navigation for ODS DOCUMENT. The navigation bookmark level is reduced to one level only. Optionally a label can be applied to all content items or the navigation label can be removed completely.
smile_ods_document_flat_label.descriptionMore: The navigation in PDF documents can be one level only with this macro. CONTENTS="" must be applied to the PROC REPORT as option and additionally for a BREAK BEFORE PAGE option.
smile_ods_document_flat_label.reference: A nice overview of ODS DOCUMENT can be found here: https://support.sas.com/resources/papers/proceedings12/273-2012.pdf
smile_ods_document_flat_label.issues: The table of contents created per default by SAS (ODS PDF option TOC) is not linking the pages correctly when using BY groups and having one ODS DOCUMENT with multiple outputs, using single ODS DOCUMENTS (one per each output) then this is working correctly. In such a case, do use not a TOC or create an own TOC, e.g. like described here: https://www.mwsug.org/proceedings/2012/S1/MWSUG-2012-S125.pdf
smile_ods_document_flat_label.author: Katja Glass

parameters.LABEL: One label to apply on first element, all other labels are removed (optional), if not provided, labels are just rearranged and additional BY-labels removed
parameters.BOOKMARKLABEL: Indicator whether to use Bookmark Labels if none is specified (YES Default), if LABEL is missing and BOOKMARKLABEL = NO, all labels are removed

smile_pdf_merge.name: smile_pdf_merge
smile_pdf_merge.description: Merge multiple PDF files and create one bookmark entry per PDF file with PROC GROOVY and open-source Tool PDFBox
smile_pdf_merge.descriptionMore: Make sure to download PDFBOX, e.g. from here https://pdfbox.apache.org/download.html - the full "app" version
smile_pdf_merge.reference: A paper explaining how to use PDFBOX with PROC GROOVY also for TOC is available in the following paper (https://www.lexjansen.com/phuse/2019/ct/CT05.pdf)
smile_pdf_merge.issues: "unable to resolve class" messages mean the PDFBOX is not provided correctly. "ERROR: PROCEDURE GROOVY cannot be used when SAS is in the lock down state." means that your SAS environment does not support PROC GROOVY, for this the macro cannot run the groovy code. "WARNUNG: Removed /IDTree from /Names dictionary, doesn't belong there" - this message is coming from PDFBox.
smile_pdf_merge.author: Katja Glass

parameters.DATA: Input dataset containing INFILE and BOOKMARK variable, INFILE containing single pdf files (one file per observation), BOOKMARK containing the corresponding bookmark label for this file
parameters.PDFBOX_JAR_PATH: Path and jar file name for PDFBOX open source tool, e.g. &path/pdfbox-app-2.0.22.jar
parameters.SOURCEFILE: Optional SAS program file where PROC GROOVY code is stored, default is TEMP (only temporary)
parameters.RUN_GROOVY: NO/YES indicator whether to run the final GROOVY code (default YES)

smile_pdf_read_bookmarks.name: smile_pdf_read_bookmarks
smile_pdf_read_bookmarks.description: Read PDF Bookmarks into a SAS dataset with the variables level, title and page
smile_pdf_read_bookmarks.descriptionMore: Make sure to download PDFBOX, e.g. from here https://pdfbox.apache.org/download.html - the full "app" version
smile_pdf_read_bookmarks.reference: PDFBox contains a lot of useful functionalities (https://pdfbox.apache.org)
smile_pdf_read_bookmarks.issues: "unable to resolve class" messages mean the PDFBOX is not provided correctly. "ERROR: PROCEDURE GROOVY cannot be used when SAS is in the lock down state." means that your SAS environment does not support PROC GROOVY, for this the macro cannot run the groovy code.
smile_pdf_read_bookmarks.author: Katja Glass

parameters.PDFFILE: name of PDF file with bookmarks
parameters.PDFBOX_JAR: path and jar file name for PDFBOX open source tool, e.g. &path/pdfbox-app-2.0.22.jar

smile_replace_in_text_files.name: smile_replace_in_text_files
smile_replace_in_text_files.description: Replace text from all files contained in a folder with a different text
smile_replace_in_text_files.author: Katja Glass

parameters.OUTPATH: output directory, if same as INPATH, files are overwritten (no quotes)
parameters.REPLACE_FROM: text which should be replaced (with quotes)
parameters.REPLACE_WITH: text that should newly be added (with quotes)
parameters.FILETYPE: extension of file types which should be processed, e.g. sas or txt (optional)

smile_url_check.name: smile_url_check
smile_url_check.description: Check existence of URL and store result in return code, information can optionally be printed to the log
smile_url_check.descriptionMore: Return codes are 0 - url found, 999 - no url provided, 998 - url not provided in quotes, otherwise html-return code (e.g. 404 file not found)
smile_url_check.reference: The idea from this macro is coming from a paper by Joseph Henry - The ABCs of PROC HTTP (https://www.sas.com/content/dam/SAS/support/en/sas-global-forum-proceedings/2019/3232-2019.pdf)
smile_url_check.author: Katja Glass

parameters.URL: http(s) URL which should be checked in quotes
parameters.RETURN: return code variable (scope should be global)
parameters.INFO: NO/YES indicator to print information to the log

smile_url_download.name: smile_url_download
smile_url_download.description: Downloads a file from an URL and store it locally on OUTFILE. Additionally return code can be stored and information can optionally be printed to the log.
smile_url_download.descriptionMore: Return codes are 0 - URL found, 999 - no URL or OUTFILE provided, 998 - URL or OUTFILE not provided in quotes, otherwise html-return code (e.g. 404 file not found)
smile_url_download.reference: The idea from this macro is coming from a paper by Joseph Henry - The ABCs of PROC HTTP (https://www.sas.com/content/dam/SAS/support/en/sas-global-forum-proceedings/2019/3232-2019.pdf)
smile_url_download.author: Katja Glass

parameters.URL: http(s) URL which should be checked in quotes
parameters.RETURN: return code variable (scope should be global)
parameters.INFO: NO/YES indicator to print information to the log
